title: [BUG] Memory leak in ExcelJS.stream.xlsx.WorkbookWriter
author: NazarKalytiuk

comments.author: seidelmartin
comments.body:
I’m experiencing the same issue; I'm not writing to the file system, but instead uploading a file directly to S3. I believe the problem lies in how streams are designed to manage the flow of data being sent upstream. In this scenario, there's a continuous stream of data being written without a mechanism to determine when to pause.

I think the `commit` method should return a boolean value: `true` or `false`. If it returns `false`, it should wait for the `drain` event to occur before resuming the writing process. This behavior is outlined in the [NodeJS Writable Stream documentation](https://nodejs.org/api/stream.html#writablewritechunk-encoding-callback).

comments.author: NazarKalytiuk

I know how to fix it (a better possible solution than I provided) without changing the library code. Will send a code snippet tomorrow.
